authors.author: William Chatterton Dix
England
Wer ist das Kind, das so friedlich schläft

properties.verseOrder: v1 v2 v3 v2 v4 v2

verse.name: v1
verse.lines: What Child is this, who, laid to rest, On Mary’s lap is sleeping? Whom angels greet with voices sweet, While shepherds watch are keeping?

verse.name: v2
verse.lines: This This is Christ, the King, Whom shepherds guard and angels sing: Haste, haste to bring Him laud, The Babe, the Son of Mary!

verse.name: v3
verse.lines: Why lies He in such mean estate, Where ox and lamb are feeding? Good Christians, fear, for sinners here The silent Word is pleading.

verse.name: v4
verse.lines: So bring Him incense, gold, and myrrh, Come, peasant, king to own Him. The King of kings salvation brings; Let loving hearts enthrone Him.
